REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE AS TO WHY DEFENDANT SHOULD NOT BE ENJOINED FROM ENFORCING THE JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO CARRY ITS BURDEN AND SHOW LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS ON ANY CAUSES OF ACTION AS ALLEGED IN THE OPERATIVE COMPLAINT. SEE KAUFFMAN V. GOLDMAN (2011) 195 CAL.APP.4TH 734. ADDITIONALLY, THE EXISTENCE OF BONA FIDE TENANCY AND APPLICABILITY OF SFRO IS NOT SHOWN BY COMPETENT, ADMISSIBLE, NON-SELF SERVING EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF ALSO FAILED TO ESTABLISH HARM THAT CANNOT BE COMPENSATED BY MONEY-DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

Order To Show Cause As To Why Defendant Should Not Be Enjoined From Enforcing The Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-12-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Mark Chernev, 415-956-8100 for Plaintiff Chris Roliz-Maguigad.
No other appearance.

Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Order To Show Cause As To Why Defendant Should Not Be Enjoined From Enforcing The Judgment is DENIED. Moving party failed to carry its burden and show likelihood of prevailing on the merits on any causes of action as alleged in the operative complaint. See Kauffman v. Goldman (2011) 195 Cal.App.4th 734. Additionally, the existence of bona fide tenancy and applicability of SFRO is not shown by competent, admissible, non-self serving evidence. Plaintiff also failed to establish harm that cannot be compensated by money-damages.
Order signed in Open Court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
